title: Operator pause and workspace ratification
summary: Operators MAY pause an active workflow at any time, and tracked changes made during that explicit pause MUST be ratified against the accepted workspace fingerprint.
instructions: An operator pause MUST preserve the prior run status, pending action, current stage, and prepared invocation until resume or an explicit stage override.
While a run is paused by the operator, the operator MAY modify tracked files in the deliverable workspace directly.

On resume, including resume with a stage override, the harness MUST compare the accepted pause-start fingerprint with the current workspace, record a ratification artifact for any pause-only delta, update the accepted workspace index and fingerprint, and invalidate stale prepared invocations.
The harness MUST NOT auto-ratify workspace divergence that existed before the operator pause began.
A normal resume MUST restore the saved status and pending action; a stage override MUST restart at that stage with prepare_invocation.
Operator pause controls MUST remain available for non-terminal runs and MUST NOT be represented as agent-owned remediation.
